name: Gluon
description: A static, type-inferred functional programming language designed for application embedding

implementation.comment: No C/C++ dependencies in the dependency tree

language-family.comment: Syntax inspired by F#, OCaml, and Haskell. Expression syntax from F#/OCaml, type syntax from Haskell

typing.comment: Hindley-Milner based type system with extensions, allowing simple and general type inference

paradigm.comment: Functional language at heart, uses strict evaluation but has explicit lazy types

first-class-functions.comment: Functions are first-class, passed with whitespace-separated arguments

pattern-matching.comment: Match expressions for unpacking variants and records, nested patterns supported

error-handling.comment: Uses Result types for error management with Ok/Err pattern matching

async-await.comment: No native async/await syntax. Uses monadic do-expressions; Rust futures can be called from gluon

iterators-generators.comment: Standard library includes iterator functions and array operations

module-system.comment: import! macro for loading external files and modules

standard-library.comment: Includes std.io, std.json, std.result, std.thread, and more

compilation-model.comment: Can evaluate at runtime or precompile; modular architecture with separate parser, typechecker, VM

hot-reloading.comment: Scripts can be reloaded; separate module compilation

source-maps.comment: Uses codespan for error reporting with source locations

startup-time.comment: Pure Rust, no JIT warmup, but type checking adds overhead

execution-speed.comment: ~250-290x slower than native Rust for function calls; ~4-10x slower than Lua in factorial benchmarks

memory-overhead.comment: Separate heaps per thread keeps individual heaps small

garbage-collection.comment: Garbage collected with separate heap per executing thread

pure-rust.comment: No C/C++ dependencies. 54K SLoC pure Rust

api-safety.comment: Unsafe contained internally; public API is safe

idiomatic-api.comment: Good type integration but requires duplicate type definitions in Rust and Gluon; API docs only 27% complete

serde-integration.comment: serde support available with custom derive macros

async-compatible.comment: Tokio integration via feature flag; Rust functions returning futures automatically suspend gluon threads

function-registration.comment: primitive! macro for function registration; VmBuilder for VM setup

type-mapping.comment: record! and record_type! macros for type mapping; serde for serialization

no-std-compatible.comment: Uses std extensively for threading and I/O

wasm-compatible.comment: Interpreter can compile to WASM (~3.5MB) but with limitations; no native WASM backend for gluon code

blank-slate-mode.comment: Base primitives are always available; sandboxing via IO monad rather than blank slate

memory-sandboxing.comment: Scripts cannot access arbitrary host memory; separate heaps per thread

memory-limits.comment: No documented memory limits; relies on separate heaps per thread

syscall-restrictions.comment: Channels and references require IO monad; side effects controlled via type system

capability-security.comment: IO monad controls side effects; scripts must explicitly use IO for effectful operations

default-deny.comment: Standard library is available by default; sandboxing via IO monad rather than capability removal

share-data.comment: Marshalling with minimal boilerplate; supports both cloning and references

custom-types.comment: Rust structs can be exposed via record macros

operator-overloading.comment: Operators are functions; custom operator definitions supported

method-syntax.comment: Record field access with dot notation

callbacks-events.comment: Functions are first-class; can be passed as callbacks

crates-io-downloads.comment: Estimated based on ~26/month current rate and project age since 2016

package-manager.comment: No package ecosystem; modules loaded via import!

editor-support.comment: Language server, VS Code extension, and Vim plugin available

example-projects.comment: Used in 9 crates; designed for application embedding and configuration

maintenance-status.comment: Last commit Dec 2022, last release Sep 2023; fixes for newer Rust versions but limited new features

documentation-quality.comment: Book available with syntax guide; API docs only 27.38% complete

maturity.comment: Usable but still v0.x; some stability issues reported (e.g., stack overflow on complex types)

primary-use-cases.comment: Designed for application embedding; static typing good for safe interfaces
